display.description: Glyphic display face for sigils, prominent headers, and runic flavor. Do not use for long text.

mono.description: Precise mono for data, logs, AI thought steps, and timelines. High developer credibility.

body.description: Humanist body for chat messages, UI labels, and descriptions. Balances the extreme display and mono.

spacing.philosophy: Compact for developer tool density, but expansive around critical sigils/actions.

radii.philosophy: Harsh, precise cuts. Avoid soft friendly corners.

motion_specs.glitch_flicker: Rapid 50ms opacity toggles between 0.8 and 1.0, paired with horizontal 1px translations. Use on state changes.
motion_specs.sigil_bloom: Scale from 0.95 to 1.05 while increasing box-shadow glow intensity, easing out over 400ms. Use on APPROVE/TAKE OVER.
motion_specs.scanline_sweep: Vertical linear gradient overlay moving top to bottom over 2s. Use during 'planning' or 'observing' AI states.
motion_specs.runic_underline: Width expansion from 0% to 100% using step-end easing (jerky/typewriter effect). Use on speech or incoming text.

council_cockpit_chat.user_bubble: Background: Tape Ash (#121214). Border: None. Text: Bone White. Font: IBM Plex Sans. Alignment: Right.
council_cockpit_chat.operator_bubble: Background: Obsidian. Border: 1px solid Phosphor Green (#61FF7E). Text: JetBrains Mono. Glow: Faint phosphor text-shadow. Alignment: Left.
council_cockpit_chat.mentor_bubble: Background: Void Black. Border: 1px solid Runic Gold (#D4AF37) with subtle hex-mesh watermark. Font: Cinzel Decorative for header, IBM Plex for body. Alignment: Center (Oracle presence).

risk_confidence_badges.style: 1px solid border matching risk color. Monospace text matching risk color. Transparent background. For Black risk, use dashed white border over pure black.
risk_confidence_badges.confidence_indicator: Mono font percentage (e.g., 'CFD: 94%'). Use Phosphor Green for >90%, Ochre for 70-89%, Oxide Red for <70%.

timeline_log_row.layout: Flex row. Timestamp (mono, ash gray) -> State Dot (color matched to ambient state) -> Action Text (body font, bone white).
timeline_log_row.style: Hovering over a row triggers a subtle 'glitch' text-shadow effect (e.g., cyan/red chromatic aberration).

floating_system_overlay.layout: Compact persistent bottom/top strip. Fixed position, z-50.
floating_system_overlay.style: Backdrop blur (backdrop-blur-md) with 80% opaque Void Black background. 1px Phosphor Green top border.
floating_system_overlay.content: Ambient State pulsing dot, current step (n/N) in JetBrains Mono, fading out previous step, typing in next step.

sovereignty_buttons.style: Harsh rectangular buttons. Uppercase JetBrains Mono with generous tracking.

variants.PAUSE: Border: Ochre. Text: Ochre. Hover: Ochre Background, Black Text.
variants.EXPLAIN: Border: Cyan. Text: Cyan. Hover: Cyan Background, Black Text.
variants.TAKE_OVER: Border: Oxide Red. Text: Oxide Red. Hover: Red Background, White Text + Glitch Flicker.
variants.APPROVE: Border: Phosphor Green. Text: Phosphor Green. Hover: Green Background, Black Text + Sigil Bloom.

imagery_direction.style: No placeholder visuals. Heavy use of abstract textures (obsidian rock, CRT scanlines, analog noise) overlaid with hex grids or runic sigils.

references.usage: Use as a very low opacity (5-10%) background texture for the entire app to kill the 'flat digital' look.
Use blended via mix-blend-mode (e.g., overlay or screen) in the Floating System Overlay or during 'TAKE OVER' actions to simulate system strain.

iconography_direction.library: Phosphor Icons (duotone or regular) for web. Material Symbols (Sharp) for Compose.
iconography_direction.style: Technical, precise, and unrounded. Avoid soft or friendly icons. Use sharp geometries to match the glitch mystic aesthetic.
iconography_direction.custom_glyphs: Use Cinzel Decorative text characters (like '∆', 'Ω', '∑') as iconography where appropriate for the Swurlz Mentor/Occult vibe.

instructions_to_main_agent: Ensure the application strictly follows the 'Glitch Mystic' aesthetic: combine brute technical UI (monospace, sharp corners, harsh borders) with subtle occult/arcane textures.
Never use Inter, Roboto, or generic sans for headers. Use the specified IBM Plex for body, JetBrains Mono for data/logs, and Cinzel Decorative ONLY for prominent mentor/system headers.
In the Council Cockpit, visually distinct chat bubbles are critical: User (muted/minimal), Operator (technical/terminal), Mentor (ornate/sigil-based).
For the Jetpack Compose app, define a custom dark color palette using the hex codes provided. Ensure all risk tier and AI ambient states are implemented as semantic color tokens.
Ensure all interactive elements have data-testid attributes formatted as kebab-case and role-based (e.g., data-testid='approve-action-button').
Do not use generic card shadows; use the defined 'glow' effects to simulate faint phosphor monitors.
Implement the 'sovereignty buttons' with exact hover/press states described to ensure the user feels authoritative control over the AI.
